ab: kataunan a muribush a thau naak a ama.
ch: 我的爸爸是部落的獵人。
en: My dad is a hunter of the tribe.

ab: tu azazakuan iaku, naak a ama numa pan’amaan mutusi hudun ya tanlhuan, kmathu thaithuy sa funush numa puthuum.
ch: 我小的時候，爸爸和叔叔會在晚上出門，帶著獵刀和獵槍。
en: When I was little, my dad and uncle would go out at night with hunting knives and guns.

ab: ya shashanuiza, kmathu thaithuy mara sa matiqtu a lhkaribush makunataun.
ch: 天亮時，他們就會帶著獵到的獵物回來。
en: At daybreak, they returned with their prey.

ab: ya tu suma wazish, ya tu suma rawaz, itia mani sa alhua masa lhizashan.
ch: 有時候是山豬、有時候是飛鼠，偶爾也會有雉雞。
en: Sometimes it was a boar, sometimes a flying squirrel, and occasionally a pheasant.

ab: mapa’inqbit thaithuy lhkaribush a bunlhaz isai kataunan a pitaw, patara thaithuy suma wa thau.
ch: 他們會在村口將獵物分好，分享給族人。
en: They would divide the prey at the entrance of the village and share it with the tribe.

ab: miaqay iaku miku untal naak a ama muribush.
ch: 我每次都會吵著爸爸，要跟著爸爸去打獵。
en: Every time I quarreled with my father, I wanted to go hunting with my father.

ab: naak a ama mzai ya mara’iniza amakathu iakin muntal muribush.
ch: 爸爸說等我長大了他再帶我一起去。
en: Dad said that he would take me with me when I grew up.

ab: kataunan a ayuzi mafazaq mani kmalawa sa tuqris, putuqris numa kmalawa qadup thaithuy ya muribush.
ch: 部落的男人也會用陷阱抓獵物，他們會放吊陷和設置石壓式陷阱。
en: The men of the tribe also trap their prey, making lasso traps and rock traps.

ab: mathuaw naak a ama ma’ania tmuqris, ama mzai, “haya wa tuqris apuqtu sa alhua, amariqaz iamin lhkaribush a sapaz, numa atmuqris iamin pisui sinaranan lhkaribush.”
ch: 我的爸爸很會設置吊陷，爸爸說:「這是用來捕抓雉雞的，我們要仔細觀察獵物的腳印，然後將吊陷放在獵物走過的路上。」
en: My dad is very good at making lasso traps. Dad said that this is used to catch pheasants. We have to carefully observe the footprints of the prey, and the traps should be placed on the path of the prey.

ab: mangqtu iaku muhiaw muntal parhaway numa tanatuqash mutusi hudun muribush, numawan tatata wa qali minfazaq iaku kmalawa sa tuqris, amuhiaw naak a tuqris shduu mara sa lhkaribush.
ch: 為了可以趕快和大人一起去打獵，我每天練習製作陷阱，很快地我的陷阱就真的可以抓到獵物了。
en: In order to go hunting with adults as soon as possible, I practiced making traps every day, and soon my traps can really catch prey.

ab: minriqaz naak a ama iakin mzai, “aminma’ania wa muribush a thau ihu ya kahiza.”
ch: 爸爸看到後對我說:「我以後一定會是很厲害的獵人。」
en: Dad saw it and told me that I will be a very good hunter in the future.

ab: mara’iniza iaku thuini, mapa iaku naak a futulh numa lhpazishan muntal naak a ama iakin musui muribush.
ch: 現在我長大了，我背起我的弓和箭矢，跟著爸爸一起去打獵。
en: Now that I have grown up, I carry my bow and arrows and go hunting with my father.

ab: mutaun iamin kataunan ya itia matiqtu sa lhkaribush patara pakaan suma wa thau.
ch: 回村時將獵到的獵物和族人們分享。
en: When returning to the village, share the hunted prey with the tribe.

ab: ama mzai apaplish iamin, piakalingkin maniun mulhthkiz tanatuqash, mathuaw maqitan pimbahi iamin muribush mara manasha wa lhkaribush.
ch: 爸爸說，我們要感謝祖靈賜福，讓我們有很多獵物收穫。
en: Dad said, we want to thank the ancestors for their blessings, so that we have a lot of prey to harvest.
